name: Quadigy
description: Quad 7-stage envelope generator with presets, live/studio modes, cycle control, and a 156-point CV matrix. It stretches to clock, morphs between saved states, and doubles as a complex multi-envelope modulation source.

controls.name: Env 1-4 Buttons
controls.description: Select one or more envelopes for editing and multi-editing.
controls.detail: hold one button and add others to edit several envelopes at once

controls.name: Preset/Edit
controls.description: Opens preset functions and editing pages.
controls.detail: load, save, clear, or edit depending on mode

controls.name: Live
controls.description: Switches between studio and live behavior.
controls.detail: long press Global to toggle live mode

controls.name: Global
controls.description: Enters global settings and mode changes.

controls.name: Cycle
controls.description: Enters cycle-related settings and clocked looping behavior.
controls.detail: cycle mode can be fit to external or internal clocks

controls.name: CV Matrix
controls.description: Enables modulation assignment and CV source selection.
controls.detail: sources: CV A, CV B, User knob

controls.name: Encoder
controls.description: Navigates menus and edits parameter pages.
controls.detail: press to edit · turn to change · press again to confirm

controls.name: Faders 1-4
controls.description: Adjust the current page's four parameters.
controls.detail: page-dependent assignments: ADSR, timing, curves, or output-related values

controls.name: User Knob
controls.description: Assignable modulation source and edit control.
controls.detail: acts as CV U in the matrix

inputs.name: G/T 1
inputs.description: Gate, trigger, or cycle input for envelope 1.

inputs.name: G/T 2
inputs.description: Gate, trigger, or cycle input for envelope 2.

inputs.name: G/T 3
inputs.description: Gate, trigger, or cycle input for envelope 3.

inputs.name: G/T 4
inputs.description: Gate, trigger, or cycle input for envelope 4.

inputs.name: Clock
inputs.description: External clock input for cycle and fit-to-clock behavior.

behaviors.name: Live and studio modes
behaviors.description: Studio mode exposes every setting. Live mode hides or simplifies editing so a performance patch can be adjusted without exposing the full complexity on stage.

behaviors.name: Multi-editing
behaviors.description: Several envelopes can be edited together as absolute values. The leftmost selected envelope drives the display, which makes polyphonic alignment fast.

behaviors.name: Manual gate
behaviors.description: The encoder button doubles as a manual gate for the selected envelopes. It is a fast way to audition a shape without an external trigger source.

behaviors.name: Cycle with fit-to-clock
behaviors.description: The module can time-stretch or compress its envelopes to fit an internal or external clock. Each channel can also be clock-divided, multiplied, or offset.

behaviors.name: Preset morphing
behaviors.description: Presets store envelope shapes and routing state, then morph over time rather than snapping. That makes the module useful for long evolving structures, not just static ADSR work.

behaviors.name: Seven-stage envelopes
behaviors.description: Each envelope can use pre-delay, hold, attack, punch, decay, sustain, and release. Curve shaping, inversion, and output level all remain independently controllable.

behaviors.name: Mod matrix routing
behaviors.description: CV A, CV B, and the User knob can be assigned in amplitude and polarity to many destinations. The matrix is deep enough to turn one envelope into a self-modulating system.

patchIdeas.name: Four-envelope drum rack
patchIdeas.patch: Four triggers into G/T 1-4 · Env 1-4 each drive a separate VCA or filter · one Quadigy covers the kick, snare, hat, and accent envelopes in a compact drum voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Live: on
* Cycle: off

Sequencer:
VCO:
VCA:
Output:

- Sequencer (Trig 1) t> Quadigy (G/T 1)
- Sequencer (Trig 2) t> Quadigy (G/T 2)
- Sequencer (Trig 3) t> Quadigy (G/T 3)
- Sequencer (Trig 4) t> Quadigy (G/T 4)
- Quadigy (Env 1) >> VCA (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 2) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Clocked cycling pad
patchIdeas.patch: Cycle mode on · external clock into Clock · Env 1 and Env 2 set to long shapes · fit-to-clock enabled · the module behaves like two tempo-locked low-frequency function generators that stay musically aligned.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Cycle: on
* Live: on

Clock:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Quadigy (Clock)
- Quadigy (Env 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Quadigy (Env 2) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Morphing swell
patchIdeas.patch: Preset A holds a slow swell · Preset B holds a fast punch · morph between them with CV A assigned to preset morph · the same chord blooms or snaps depending on where the matrix sits.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Preset: A
* CV Matrix: preset morph
* Cycle: off

Sequencer:
VCO:
VCA:
Output:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> Quadigy (G/T 1)
- Quadigy (Env 1) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Polyphonic multi-edit
patchIdeas.patch: Select Env 1-4 together and push the same curve or timing changes across all four channels · one set of gestures keeps a four-voice patch aligned while still leaving the outputs independent.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Live: off
* Cycle: off

VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
VCO 4:
VCA:
VCA 2:
VCA 3:
VCA 4:
Mixer:
Output:

- Quadigy (Env 1) >> VCA (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 2) >> VCA 2 (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 3) >> VCA 3 (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 4) >> VCA 4 (CV)
- VCO 1 (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> VCA 2 (In)
- VCO 3 (Out) -> VCA 3 (In)
- VCO 4 (Out) -> VCA 4 (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Mixer (In 1)
- VCA 2 (Out) -> Mixer (In 2)
- VCA 3 (Out) -> Mixer (In 3)
- VCA 4 (Out) -> Mixer (In 4)
- Mixer (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Envelope chain
patchIdeas.patch: Use EOR or EOF-style linking in the envelope settings and let one channel trigger the next · the module becomes a complex attack engine that steps through related stages without a separate logic module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Live: on
* Cycle: on

Clock:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Quadigy (G/T 1)
- Quadigy (Env 1) >> Quadigy (CV A)
- Quadigy (Env 2) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Quadigy (Env 3) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
